ai_strategy_lead.name: Jasbir Dhaliwal
ai_strategy_lead.title: Executive Vice President for Research and Innovation

documents.title: UofM Making $1M Investment to Further Enhance Technology Innovation Ecosystem (strategic announcement aligned with xAI's arrival)
Mid-South AI Research Consortium founding agreement

ai_strategy.summary: University of Memphis has not published a single formal institutional AI strategy document, but AI is a stated strategic priority. In June 2024 the university announced a $1M internal investment to expand AI and data science research, degree programs, and stackable micro-credentials in alignment with xAI's Colossus supercomputer arriving in Memphis. In April 2026 UofM co-founded the Mid-South AI Research Consortium to coordinate AI research, workforce development and joint federal funding across four R1 universities.

key_provisions: Instructor-driven discretion to permit, restrict, or prohibit generative AI per course
Student disclosure of AI use required via citation or written acknowledgement
Syllabus-level policy statements required, with four UM3D-supplied templates (prohibit, directed-use, permission-based, disclosure)
Undisclosed AI use treated as Academic Integrity Policy violation
Responsible integration framed around ethical standards, data privacy, and academic integrity
ai_policy.summary: University of Memphis follows an instructor-driven, permissive approach codified in Tennessee Public Chapter 550 requirements and published via UM3D syllabus templates and college-level policies (e.g., FCBE adopted July 2025). Faculty must include a syllabus-level AI statement picking from prohibit/directed-use/permission/disclosure models. Students must disclose any AI use; non-disclosure is treated as academic misconduct.

bodies.name: AI Community of Practice (UM3D)
bodies.scope: Faculty/staff forum on AI in teaching and learning; founded Fall 2023; co-led 2025-2026 by Amanda Savage and Emily Gillo

bodies.name: FedEx Institute of Technology AI Research Cluster
bodies.scope: University-wide AI research coordination hub housing CERTAIN and AI Research Cluster

bodies.name: Division of Research & Innovation (EVP Jasbir Dhaliwal)
bodies.scope: Oversees AI research strategy, consortium participation, and AI research cluster investment

governance_structure.summary: UofM has no single standing AI council, chief AI officer, or presidential task force. Governance is distributed across the Division of Research & Innovation (under EVP Jasbir Dhaliwal), the FedEx Institute of Technology (which houses CERTAIN and the AI Research Cluster), and the UM3D AI Community of Practice for teaching and learning. AI policy authority sits at the college/department level under Tennessee state law.

initiatives.title: Mid-South AI Research Consortium founding
initiatives.description: On April 20, 2026, University of Memphis joined University of Arkansas, University of Mississippi and University of Tennessee Health Science Center to launch the Mid-South AI Research Consortium, a four-university R1 partnership with several hundred AI researchers. The consortium targets five pillars: rural and precision health, supply chain and logistics, energy and data centers, agriculture and food security, and national defense, and runs on a low-overhead model to enable joint federal grant applications.
initiatives.significance: Creates one of the first formal regional R1 consortia aimed at closing the gap between fragmented Mid-South AI capabilities and peer coastal AI hubs, with workforce development and shared compute as co-equal goals.

investment.funding_source: Joint federal grant pursuit; institutional contributions from four partner universities
investment.description: Founding covenant commits partners to shared digital environments, faculty/student exchange, and coordinated pursuit of federal AI grants. Dollar figure not disclosed at launch.

partners: University of Tennessee Health Science Center
collaborations.description: Four-university R1 consortium founded April 20, 2026 in Memphis.

initiatives.policy_implications: Regional R1s positioning AI as a shared workforce and economic-development priority rather than competing silos.

initiatives.title: $1M AI and Technology Innovation Ecosystem investment
initiatives.description: In June 2024, President Bill Hardgrave announced a $1 million institutional investment to expand the university's AI and technology research and education capacity, in direct alignment with xAI's Colossus supercomputer arriving in Memphis. Funds seeded the AI Research Cluster, Data Science Research Cluster, Learner Data Institute, Center for Electrified and Autonomous Transportation, Agile Freight Supply Chain (NSF IUCRC), 901 AI community engagement, and expanded degree programs and stackable micro-credentials.
initiatives.significance: Signals a mid-sized public R1 positioning itself as the regional talent and research anchor for a rapidly growing commercial AI supercluster in its own backyard.

investment.funding_source: University of Memphis internal strategic investment
investment.description: $1M institutional seed across seven named AI/technology programs; paired with commitment to degree expansion and stackable micro-credentials.

collaborations.description: Ecosystem alignment with xAI's Memphis Colossus facility; no formal MOU disclosed publicly.

initiatives.policy_implications: Public R1 leveraging nearby private-sector AI infrastructure without requiring a formal vendor partnership to expand workforce programs.

initiatives.title: AI for All interdisciplinary minor
initiatives.description: University of Memphis launched the AI for All minor in Fall 2025, a 12-credit interdisciplinary minor open to all majors and requiring no technical background. It is built around two core courses (AIFA 1000 Artificial Intelligence Literacy and AIFA 2010 Skills and Techniques for Applied AI) plus two electives from anthropology, film, geospatial analysis, business, education, healthcare, philosophy, public health, or language learning.
initiatives.significance: One of a small but growing number of explicit AI-literacy-for-all-majors offerings at public R1s; institutionalizes AI literacy as cross-disciplinary rather than locking it inside CS.

investment.description: Seeded by the 2024 $1M AI investment; administered by the AI for All program office.

collaborations.description: Cross-college collaboration internal to UofM (A&S, FCBE, Education, Public Health, Communication & Fine Arts).

initiatives.policy_implications: Validates the thesis that AI literacy belongs in the general/liberal-education layer, not just CS.

initiatives.title: CERTAIN — Center for Emerging Technologies in Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: CERTAIN is the FedEx Institute of Technology's research center addressing the 'capability-deployment gap' between AI capabilities and organizational implementation across six sectors: healthcare, manufacturing, logistics, higher education, supply chain, and software development. Led by three faculty co-directors (Srikar Velichety, Xiaolei Huang, Bernie Daigle Jr.) with partners including FedEx, Institute of Applied AI and Robotics, U Missouri-St. Louis, UMass Amherst, and Walter Reed Army Institute of Research.
initiatives.significance: Deployment-focused applied AI center is a distinctive posture versus peer R1s that emphasize foundational research; demonstrates a pragmatic industry-facing model.

investment.funding_source: FedEx Institute of Technology, federal and corporate sponsors
investment.description: Undisclosed; center operates through FedEx Institute with sponsored-research model.

partners: Institute of Applied AI and Robotics (IAAIR)
Walter Reed Army Institute of Research
collaborations.description: Multi-sector R&D partnerships spanning Fortune 500, government, and peer universities.

initiatives.policy_implications: Research center explicitly addressing governance, human factors, and safety protocols alongside capability research.

initiatives.title: iTiger NSF-funded GPU cluster
initiatives.description: UofM deployed the iTiger GPU cluster in October 2024 — the first regional mid-scale GPU cluster in the Mid-South — funded by a $1.1M NSF Major Research Instrumentation grant led by PI Xiaolei Huang with co-PIs McKenna, Preza, Rus, and Kumar. Cluster has 928 AMD CPU cores, 88 NVIDIA GPUs (Ada 5000, Ada 6000, H100) across 11 nodes, 233 TB SSD, and 1.32 PB HDD storage, supporting AI, NLP, bioinformatics, precision agriculture, and health informatics across Mid-South institutions.
initiatives.significance: Places a Carnegie R1 Mid-South university at the center of regional AI compute sharing, a model for how mid-tier R1s can serve as compute-access anchors.

investment.funding_source: National Science Foundation Major Research Instrumentation (NSF MRI) grant
investment.description: $1.1M NSF MRI grant to Xiaolei Huang et al. to build and operate iTiger GPU cluster for Mid-South research institutions.

collaborations.description: NSF MRI program funded cluster acquisition and deployment.

initiatives.policy_implications: NSF-funded regional compute-sharing models are a template for addressing compute inequality between flagship and mid-size R1s.

initiatives.title: Instructor-driven syllabus-level AI policy (FCBE + UM3D templates)
initiatives.description: In response to Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024), UofM adopted an instructor-driven AI policy framework. The Fogelman College of Business and Economics (FCBE) adopted its formal AI policy in July 2025, and UM3D published four syllabus-level template statements (prohibit, directed-use, permission-based, disclosure) that every faculty member must adapt. Students must acknowledge any AI use in writing or via citation; non-disclosure is treated as an Academic Integrity Policy violation.
initiatives.significance: A defensive policy posture that satisfies Tennessee state law without centralizing AI authority — decentralized model increasingly common among mid-size public R1s.

investment.description: Policy development effort; no dedicated budget disclosed.

collaborations.description: Compliance with Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024), which required higher-ed governing boards to adopt AI policies by July 1, 2025.

initiatives.policy_implications: Instructor-driven, disclosure-centric approach is the emerging mainstream for non-UT-System Tennessee public universities.

initiatives.title: AI Community of Practice (UM3D)
initiatives.description: UM3D's AI Community of Practice, founded Fall 2023, is a faculty/staff forum meeting roughly three times per semester to explore LLMs and generative AI in teaching and learning. It runs a self-enrolled Canvas course ('AI for Teaching & Learning'), a resources page, book clubs, and working groups. 2025-2026 co-leaders are Amanda Savage and Emily Gillo.
initiatives.significance: An example of a grassroots faculty-driven AI adoption model, complementary to top-down strategy, and characteristic of mid-size R1s without chief AI officers.

investment.description: Faculty-development center activity with no standalone budget disclosed.

tools_and_vendors.name: Canvas
tools_and_vendors.role: Delivery platform for 'AI for Teaching & Learning' self-enrolled course

tools_and_vendors.name: Khanmigo
tools_and_vendors.role: Example AI tutoring tool discussed in book-club/workshop

initiatives.policy_implications: Grassroots faculty communities of practice are an efficient early-stage substitute for formal AI governance at mid-size R1s.

initiatives.title: AI for Health Research Lab (AIHR) — NIH AIM-AHEAD PAIR grant
initiatives.description: The School of Public Health's AI for Health Research Lab, directed by Prof. Ricky Leung, was established through NIH's AIM-AHEAD Consortium under the Program for Artificial Intelligence Readiness (PAIR). PAIR Phase 1 provides up to $100K for institutional teaming and training and Phase 2 up to $150K to launch the research lab. Focus areas: substance use disorders, maternal and child health, mental health, aging, nutrition, and disease prevention using ethically grounded AI.
initiatives.significance: Positions UofM inside the federal AIM-AHEAD network focused on diversifying AI/ML workforce and addressing health disparities — a distinctive equity angle.

investment.funding_source: NIH AIM-AHEAD Consortium — Program for Artificial Intelligence Readiness (PAIR), Phases 1 and 2
investment.description: Combined Phase 1 ($100K) plus Phase 2 ($150K) AIM-AHEAD PAIR award supporting lab launch and exploratory projects.

initiatives.policy_implications: Embeds equity and ethical-AI framing inside a federally funded R1 health research lab.

initiatives.title: Generative AI mental-health chatbot for student stress management
initiatives.description: UofM researchers Polina Durneva, Yuan Zhang (FCBE) and Sohye Lee (Loewenberg College of Nursing) are developing a generative AI chatbot trained on NIH National Institute of Mental Health and American Psychological Association materials to deliver tailored stress self-management to students as a scalable complement to traditional counseling services.
initiatives.significance: Example of faculty-led, research-grade student-facing AI addressing mental-health access — distinct from vendor-licensed campus chatbots.

collaborations.description: Cross-college faculty research collaboration internal to UofM.

initiatives.policy_implications: Positions research-grade AI chatbots as an access-expansion tool for mental-health services, with ethics/data-training transparency as a differentiator.

initiatives.title: UofM–Ole Miss Collaborative AI Research Funding Opportunity
initiatives.description: University of Memphis and University of Mississippi launched a joint AI research funding call in December 2025 inviting faculty proposals to develop or apply AI for regional and national priorities. The mechanism precedes and foreshadows the April 2026 Mid-South AI Research Consortium.
initiatives.significance: Bilateral research-seed mechanism between two public R1s to de-risk cross-institution AI work ahead of a larger consortium.

investment.funding_source: Joint institutional seed funding from UofM and University of Mississippi
investment.description: Internal seed-funding mechanism; amounts per award not publicly disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Bilateral seed mechanisms are a lightweight precursor to formal multi-institution AI consortia.

initiatives.title: Computer Science AI Concentration and Graduate Certificates in Intelligence Engineering and Data Science
initiatives.description: UofM offers a B.S. in Computer Science with an Artificial Intelligence concentration, a graduate certificate in Intelligence Engineering and Applications, and a graduate certificate in Data Science, plus an MS in Data Science (33 credits, with the capstone project made optional in Fall 2024). These programs feed the stackable micro-credential commitment from the 2024 $1M investment.
initiatives.significance: Ties discrete AI-aligned academic programs to the explicit workforce-development pitch made in the $1M announcement.

investment.description: Standing academic programs; enrollment in AI-aligned CS departments has doubled over the past three years per the $1M announcement.

collaborations.description: Internal programs across CS, Data Science, and FCBE.

initiatives.policy_implications: Doubling of AI-aligned CS enrollment is consistent with the national pattern and supports the workforce-development narrative.

research_centers.focus: Language, Learning, AI — foundational AI, intelligent tutoring systems (AutoTutor, AutoMentor, MoFaCTs), cognitive science

research_centers.focus: Ethically grounded AI/ML for public-health outcomes — substance use, maternal health, mental health, aging

research_centers.name: FedEx Institute of Technology — AI Research Cluster
research_centers.focus: Large faculty AI research cluster, host for CERTAIN and interdisciplinary AI projects

research_centers.name: Learner Data Institute
research_centers.focus: AI and learning science / educational data mining

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: iTiger GPU Cluster (primary AI); BigBlue Cluster (general HPC)
computing_infrastructure.summary: UofM operates two research-computing assets: the legacy BigBlue HPC cluster (maintained since 2006) and iTiger, an NSF-MRI-funded GPU cluster deployed October 2024 with 88 NVIDIA GPUs (H100, Ada 5000/6000) on 11 nodes (928 AMD CPU cores, 233 TB SSD, 1.32 PB HDD). iTiger is described as the first regional mid-scale GPU cluster in the Mid-South and is explicitly positioned to serve Mid-South institutions for AI, NLP, precision agriculture, smart transportation, and health informatics research.

investments_and_gifts.purpose: Acquire and deploy regional GPU cluster for AI, NLP, bioinformatics, precision agriculture, health informatics

investments_and_gifts.title: NIH AIM-AHEAD PAIR Phase 1+2 award — AI for Health Research Lab
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Launch AI for Health Research Lab, train workforce, run exploratory projects in substance-use and public-health AI

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI for All minor (no prerequisites, no technical background required)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Open to any major, designed to widen AI literacy across humanities, arts, social sciences, and STEM.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: AIM-AHEAD's mandate is to diversify AI/ML researchers and address health disparities; AIHR lab anchors that work at UofM.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: iTiger regional GPU cluster
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Mid-South institutions without in-house GPU capacity can access shared HPC resources, partially offsetting regional compute inequality.

regulatory_context.summary: Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024), codified at Tenn. Code Ann. § 49-7-185, requires every state public university governing board (UT System, TBR, and Local Governing Boards — the latter includes University of Memphis) to adopt an AI policy for students, faculty, and staff by July 1, 2025. The UT System adopted BT0035 effective February 28, 2025; that policy does NOT cover UofM (UofM has been a Local Governing Board institution since leaving the Tennessee Board of Regents in 2016). UofM's compliance is handled at the college/department level via UM3D syllabus templates and college-specific policies (e.g., FCBE's July 2025 policy).

narrative_summary:
University of Memphis is a public Carnegie R1 research university (19,652 enrolled Fall 2025) that has emerged as the academic anchor of the rapidly growing Memphis-area AI ecosystem — most visibly xAI's Colossus supercomputer — without adopting the top-down governance model common at peer R1s. There is no chief AI officer and no single published institutional AI strategy; instead, AI leadership is distributed across EVP for Research and Innovation Jasbir Dhaliwal, CIO Jeff Delaney, Provost David Russomanno, and President Bill Hardgrave, with operational execution concentrated in the FedEx Institute of Technology and the Division of Research & Innovation.

The research posture is strong for a mid-size R1. IIS (founded 1985) is a long-standing AI/learning-sciences center, and it is now joined by CERTAIN (deployment-focused applied AI across six sectors) and the AI for Health Research Lab (NIH AIM-AHEAD PAIR-funded, public-health AI). The October 2024 launch of iTiger — an $1.1M NSF-MRI-funded GPU cluster with 88 NVIDIA GPUs including H100s, positioned as the first regional mid-scale cluster for the Mid-South — gives UofM rare compute-anchor status among non-flagship R1s.

The institution's most consequential 2026 move is founding the Mid-South AI Research Consortium with the University of Arkansas, University of Mississippi, and University of Tennessee Health Science Center on April 20, 2026 — a four-R1 partnership targeting rural/precision health, supply chain/logistics, energy/data centers, agriculture, and national defense. This follows a December 2025 bilateral seed-funding program with Ole Miss and the June 2024 $1M internal investment aligned with xAI's Memphis presence.

On policy, UofM takes a permissive, instructor-driven approach rooted in Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024) compliance. UM3D publishes four syllabus-template options (prohibit / directed-use / permission-based / disclosure). The Fogelman College of Business adopted its AI policy in July 2025; students must disclose AI use or face academic-integrity sanctions. An AI Community of Practice (founded 2023) provides grassroots faculty development.

Teaching and workforce are priorities: the Fall 2025 AI for All minor opens AI literacy to all undergraduate majors, paired with existing CS AI concentrations, an MS in Data Science, and graduate certificates in Intelligence Engineering and Data Science. Gaps: UofM has no publicly announced enterprise AI platform (Copilot/ChatGPT Edu) deployment, no formal AI strategy document, and no standing cross-campus AI council; enterprise adoption appears to lag research and curricular investment.

gaps_acknowledged: No formal, campus-wide published AI strategy document; derived from strategic announcements and investment press
No identified chief AI officer or dedicated presidential AI task force
CERTAIN center founding year not publicly disclosed; director structure is three faculty co-directors
No publicly disclosed enterprise AI platform deployment (Microsoft Copilot, Google Gemini, ChatGPT Edu, Claude for Education) at campus-wide scale
Mid-South AI Research Consortium funding figures not disclosed at April 2026 launch
No public information on AI use in admissions, enrollment-prediction, or retention analytics at UofM
Limited disclosure of internal AI governance committees beyond the UM3D Community of Practice
No public data on student-facing chatbot production deployments (the mental-health chatbot is a research prototype)

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: workforce regional development.
